vocab.meaning: nghi thức chải chuốt/vệ sinh cá nhân
vocab.example: Checking my reflection is a fundamental part of my morning grooming ritual before I head to the office.

vocab.meaning: một cái nhìn lướt qua nhanh chóng
vocab.example: I usually just give myself a cursory glance in the hallway mirror to ensure my collar is straight.
I tend to avoid mirrors when I'm feeling self-conscious about a breakout or a bad hair day.

vocab.meaning: bị thúc đẩy bởi lòng tự mãn/kiêu hãnh
vocab.example: I try not to be too vanity-driven, so I only look in the mirror when it's strictly necessary for functional reasons.

vocab.meaning: bị bận tâm quá mức bởi...
vocab.example: Many teenagers are preoccupied with their image, leading them to spend hours in front of a looking glass.

vocab.meaning: duy trì một hình ảnh bóng bẩy/chỉnh chu
vocab.example: As a public speaker, it is crucial for me to maintain a polished image, which requires frequent mirror checks.

vocab.meaning: vô tình nhìn thấy (hình ảnh phản chiếu)
vocab.example: I sometimes inadvertently catch sight of my reflection in shop windows while walking down the street.
I mostly use the mirror in the lift just to fix my attire and make sure everything looks presentable.

vocab.meaning: hình ảnh phản chiếu bị biến dạng
vocab.example: I don't like looking into antique mirrors because they often provide a distorted reflection that makes me feel uneasy.

vocab.meaning: cải thiện lòng tự trọng/sự tự tin
vocab.example: Sometimes, practicing positive affirmations in front of a mirror can significantly boost your self-esteem.
